articles.title: Japan PM Takaichi in Delhi from July 1-3 for annual summit: MEA
articles.description: According to a press release issued by the MEA on Friday, Prime Minister Takaichi will visit Delhi at the invitation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi from July 1 to 3, during which she will take part in the annual summit.

articles.title: Life advice of the day by Helen Keller: 'Life is a succession of lessons which must be...'- A timeless life lesson on why life must be lived before it can truly be understood by a woman of astounding intelligence
articles.description: Helen Keller's profound life lesson, Life is a succession of lessons which must be lived to be understood, underscores that true wisdom stems from experience, not just knowledge. Overcoming blindness and deafness, Keller transformed immense challenges into a life of purpose, advocating for others. Her journey highlights how resilience, gratitude, and strength are forged through hardship, urging us to embrace life's unfolding journey and learn from every experience.

articles.title: Ras Laffan accident: Mortal remains of all 12 Indians repatriated
articles.description: The mortal remains of all 12 Indian nationals who died in a tragic accident in Ras Laffan, Qatar, have been repatriated to India. The Indian Embassy in Doha confirmed the process was completed in two batches on June 25 and June 26.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Reece James out for England's match against Panama
articles.description: Thomas Tuchel has confirmed that Reece James will miss England's final World Cup group match against Panama due to a hamstring injury. However, Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice, and Elliot Anderson are available for the crucial qualifier.

While Gisele Bundchen recently sparked outrage as she snubbed Tom Brady on Father’s Day, the Brazilian supermodel has now reflected on the life she shared with the NFL legend.

While he has been a force to be reckoned with on the field, Patrick Mahomes has often spoken about how his mother, Randi Mahomes, instilled confidence in him from a young age and has always been there for him through thick and thin.

articles.title: Weekly Horoscope for ♊ GEMINI (June 27 - July 3, 2026): Powerful Networking Opportunities Could Accelerate Your Growth This Week
articles.description: Gemini Weekly Horoscope (27th June 2026 to 3rd July 2026): Gemini, this week brings mental clarity, exciting opportunities, and a stronger sense of purpose. You may notice that situations which previously felt uncertain now begin moving in a positive direction. Your natural intelligence and communication skills become powerful tools for creating progress. New ideas, meetings, conversations, or unexpected opportunities may play an important role in shaping your next steps. This is a week where being open-minded and adaptable works strongly in your favor. Trust your ability to learn quickly and adjust to changing circumstances. The opportunities that appear now could have long-term benefits. Discover what Sandeep Agarwal of Meditate Wisdom reveals in this weekly horoscope for gemini, offering valuable insights into this gemini weekly horoscope.

articles.title: ICAI to help Tirupati temple enhance accounting system
articles.description: India's wealthiest religious trust, Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams, is partnering with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) to enhance its donation record-keeping. This move follows scrutiny of religious trusts amid embezzlement allegations at the Ayodhya Ram temple. ICAI is developing a comprehensive accounting framework for TTD, expected within 100 days, which other religious bodies can also adopt.

articles.title: India-UK FTA must drive transformational growth, not just higher trade: Piyush Goyal
articles.description: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal urged Indian and British businesses to aim for "transformational growth" with the upcoming Free Trade Agreement, effective July 15. He emphasized exploring partnerships to maximize opportunities beyond incremental gains. Goyal also highlighted the Double Contribution Convention, benefiting temporary workers, and criticized global rating agencies for their assessment of India's economic potential.
